query: Designs and the Figma file keep getting reorganized and we keep re-touching code for nothing. Snapshot it into specs that survive the moves.
note: File reorg causing churn + anchor specs to stable identity = the exact pain figma-specs solves.

query: Set up living design specs from our Figma file keyed to node IDs and component keys, not frame names.
note: Establish the spec set anchored on stable ids/keys = figma-specs init.

query: Track design drift between Figma and the code and tell me which changes actually need a re-touch.
note: Detect drift + separate reorg-noise from real changes = figma-specs refresh/drift report.

query: Re-read the Figma file, diff against our snapshot, and give me the drift report split into reorg-only vs real token changes.
note: Refresh + by-stable-id diff + two-list drift report is figma-specs verbatim.

query: Sync our design tokens from Figma to code and catch the small spacing/color deltas before they pile up.
note: Sync design to code + catch accumulating token deltas = figma-specs's variable diffing.

query: Build a design-to-code map from our Figma components to the React components in the repo and store it in the spec.
note: Repo-owned design→code map discovered by name-matching = figma-specs core mechanism.

query: Refresh the design specs after the designers moved everything around — I don't want to re-touch code for pure moves.
note: Refresh that flags moves as no-op (same id, new locator) = figma-specs's whole point.

query: Whenever the Figma file changes I want to know what genuinely changed versus what just got renamed or moved.
note: Renamed/moved vs changed classification by stable id = figma-specs drift table.

query: Establish the design spec set for our component library and report how many components are mapped to code vs unmapped.
note: Init that reports mapped-vs-unmapped components = figma-specs init deliverable.

query: Map this codebase into living specs so new engineers can onboard — deep-dive the domains and conventions.
note: Mapping the CODEBASE into specs = spelunking-specs; figma-specs is about design drift, not code archaeology.

query: Recon the repo and write spec docs that flag where the code drifts from its own patterns.
note: Code-drift specs from a codebase deep-dive = spelunking-specs, despite the 'specs'/'drift' overlap.

query: Generate React code from this Figma frame I selected.
note: One-shot design-to-code generation from a frame = Figma's /figma-generate-design, not drift-tracking specs.

query: Connect to Figma and pull the layout, typography, and colors of this screen for reference.
note: Plain read of design context = the figma pointer skill, not building/refreshing a drift spec set.

query: Set up Code Connect to link our Figma components to their source files.
note: Code Connect setup is its own Figma skill; figma-specs treats Code Connect as optional, not the task.

query: Authenticate the Figma MCP — my token isn't connecting.
note: MCP auth/connection troubleshooting = the figma pointer skill, not spec authoring.

query: Spec out our public REST API into documentation for external developers.
note: API documentation authoring — 'spec' keyword but no Figma/design drift involved.

query: Reproduce in the browser why the button's hover state looks wrong on production.
note: Live-browser repro of a rendering bug = browser-diagnosis, not comparing Figma to code.

query: Create a brand-new Figma file and scaffold a starter design system in it.
note: Authoring a new Figma file = a Figma write skill, not snapshotting an existing file into drift specs.

query: Convert this spreadsheet of survey results into a polished chart.
note: Truly unrelated — spreadsheet/charting work, nothing to do with Figma or design specs.
